options.label: An AI-powered development environment that helps developers design, implement, and test software
An assistant that only autocompletes code snippets line by line as you type
A chat extension that answers code questions but cannot edit files or execute commands
A CI/CD platform that automates builds, tests, and deploys for the project

questions.explanation: Kiro is an intelligent AI-powered development environment that works alongside developers to design systems, explore solutions, and implement code efficiently.

questions.text: Kiro is capable of editing files and executing terminal commands directly in the development environment.
questions.explanation: Kiro has file editing capabilities, terminal command execution, and CLI automation assistance, working directly in the developer's environment.

questions.text: You are starting a new project and want Kiro to help structure the architecture. Which approach is most appropriate?

options.label: Ask Kiro to create a Spec with requirements, design, and tasks before implementing
Ask for a Spec with only the task list, skipping requirements and design to start coding sooner
Create a Steering file with the desired architecture and request direct implementation without a Spec
Activate an architecture Skill and ask it to generate all the code at once without incremental steps

questions.explanation: Kiro works best when used to plan before implementing. The Specs workflow allows defining requirements, creating a design, and generating incremental tasks, resulting in more structured and correct code.

questions.text: You need to develop a feature involving complex business rules. Arrange the Kiro workflow steps in the correct order:

options.label: Create the design document with architecture

questions.explanation: The Kiro workflow follows a structured progression: first define requirements, then create the design, generate tasks, and finally execute each task with continuous verification.
